standard_description: Understand and use question words (interrogatives) (e.g., who, what, where, when, why, how).
subject: Language

khan_exercises.title: The question word
khan_exercises.description: Find the question words in these sentences!

items.question_content:
**Choose the best question word for this sentence!**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_  many of us are going?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Question words ask about people (who/which), places (where), times (when), or ideas and explanations (why/what/how).
The conventional phrase for asking a question about numbers is **"how many"**.

The conventional word for asking a question about places is **"where"**.

items.question_content:
**Choose the best personal pronoun for this sentence.**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_ have to get in the truck.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Personal pronouns refer to people and things. They include *I/me, you, he/she, they, we/us, and it*.

While multiple choices may make sense in context, only one of them uses a personal pronoun.
Of the choices, only "**we**" is a personal pronoun.
